headline: ConocoPhillips Insider Sale Prompts Fresh Look At Long Term Story
summary: ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP) director Timothy Leach sold 40,000 company shares in a recent insider transaction. The sale represents a sizeable disposal by a board member and is disclosed through standard regulatory filings. Investors often review transactions of this scale for clues about insider sentiment toward the stock. ConocoPhillips is a major independent exploration and production company focused on oil and natural gas. For readers following NYSE:COP, an insider sale of 40,000 shares by...

headline: Oil Prices Near $90 Amid Latest Iran Threat; S&P 500 Slips
summary: Oil prices rose back above $90 a barrel before pulling back on Tuesday morning, just a hint of Monday's volatility that saw oil spike to $119 then plunge as President Donald Trump provided assurance that the war would end very soon.  Iran pushed back on Tuesday morning, vowing to prevent "the export of even a single liter of oil from the region to the hostile side and its partners until further notice," the Associated Press reported.  In recent hours, Iran has launched new missile and drone attacks on Israel, the United Arab Emirates and Qatar.

headline: Oil Prices Bounce After U.S. Beats Iran Blockade — Or Did It?
summary: Oil prices dived below $80 a barrel Tuesday afternoon, then rebounded after Energy Secretary Chris Wright deleted his post claiming that the U.S. Navy "successfully escorted an oil tanker through the Strait of Hormuz."  Oil prices had climbed back above $90 early in the day after Iran vowed to prevent "the export of even a single liter of oil from the region to the hostile side and its partners until further notice," as the Associated Press reported.

headline: 43 Years of Dividend Growth Keeps XOM Bulls Holding Through the Noise
summary: One of the biggest names in the oil world, shares of ExxonMobil (NYSE:XOM) are trading near $150 as retail sentiment on Reddit cools from a late-February peak. The proprietary sentiment score hit 72 on February 20, then pulled back to 46 as of March 10, sliding from bullish to neutral territory. headline: Oil Prices Bounce After Fake News As Blockade Holds; S&P 500 Fades
summary: Oil prices dived below $80 a barrel Tuesday afternoon, then rebounded after Energy Secretary Chris Wright deleted his post claiming that the U.S. Navy "successfully escorted an oil tanker through the Strait of Hormuz."  The White House later confirmed that Wright's post was false.

headline: Is ExxonMobil Poised to Gain From Current Elevation in Crude Prices?
summary: XOM stands to gain as WTI hovers near $90 per barrel, with low-cost Permian and Guyana assets boosting margins and cash flow to fund projects and expand output.

content.title: Is ExxonMobil Poised to Gain From Current Elevation in Crude Prices?

headline: Oil Prices Bounce As Iran Blockade Holds; S&P 500 Slips
summary: Oil prices dived below $80 a barrel Tuesday afternoon, then rebounded after Energy Secretary Chris Wright deleted his post claiming that the U.S. Navy "successfully escorted an oil tanker through the Strait of Hormuz."  The S&P 500 relinquished gains on the false claim and turned slightly lower.  The White House later confirmed that Wright's post was false.

content.title: Oil Prices Bounce As Iran Blockade Holds; S&P 500 Slips
content.summary: Oil prices dived below $80 a barrel Tuesday afternoon, then rebounded after Energy Secretary Chris Wright deleted his post claiming that the U.S. Navy "successfully escorted an oil tanker through the Strait of Hormuz."  The S&P 500 relinquished gains on the false claim and turned slightly lower.  The White House later confirmed that Wright's post was false.

headline: Exclusive-Chevron, Shell closing in on first big oil production deals in Venezuela since US captured Maduro, sources say
summary: HOUSTON/CARACAS, March 10 (Reuters) - International oil majors Chevron and Shell are closing in on the first big oil production deals with Venezuela since the U. capture of President Nicolas Maduro in January, five sources close to the negotiations told Reuters.

content.title: Exclusive-Chevron, Shell closing in on first big oil production deals in Venezuela since US captured Maduro, sources say
content.summary: HOUSTON/CARACAS, March 10 (Reuters) - International oil majors Chevron and Shell are closing in on the first big oil production deals with Venezuela since the U. capture of President Nicolas Maduro in January, five sources close to the negotiations told Reuters.
